title: Helix Robotics v. Carter — trade secret misappropriation by former engineer
claims: misappropriation of trade secrets (Arkansas Trade Secrets Act)
relief_sought: Preliminary and permanent injunction barring use or disclosure of misappropriated files
Compensatory damages and unjust enrichment of at least $1.2M
Exemplary damages and attorney fees under the Arkansas Trade Secrets Act
Order requiring return and forensic deletion of all confidential materials
key_facts: Defendant served as senior controls engineer from 2021 until resignation on November 1, 2025.
Forensic review of company laptop shows 4,200 files copied to a personal USB drive in the 14 days before resignation.
Files include proprietary CAD drawings and motor-tuning parameters protected by access controls and an NDA signed at hire.
Defendant joined a direct competitor on November 7, 2025 and demonstrated functionally identical robot behavior at a December trade show.

contract_clauses.name: Confidentiality
contract_clauses.text: Employee shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not use such information for any purpose other than performance of duties for the Company.

contract_clauses.name: Return of materials
contract_clauses.text: Upon termination, Employee shall promptly return all Company property and permanently delete any Confidential Information from personal devices.
